entry.title: Get BP contact address details with API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
entry.content:
BP DIGITS has contact PHONE_NUMBER with an address with all the details (e-mail, fax, telephone, mobile phone; see attached document). We cannot find a way to retrieve these details with REST API API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.


 


We are calling GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BPContactToAddress?$filter=BusinessPartnerCompany eq 'DIGITS'&$expand=to_EmailAddress,to_FaxNumber,to_MobilePhoneNumber,to_PhoneNumber


But in the response we just receive empty arrays:


"to_EmailAddress": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_FaxNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_MobilePhoneNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },
                "to_PhoneNumber": {
                    "results": []
                },


Can you instruct us what is the right way to retrieve the data?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Get BP contact address details with API_BUSINESS_PARTNER
entry.content:
Please try the url:
/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartnerContact(RelationshipNumber='xxx',BusinessPartnerCompany='xxxxx',BusinessPartnerPerson='xxxx',ValidityEndDate=datetime'9999-12-31T00:00:00')/to_ContactRelationship?$format=json

entry.title: Re: Equipment installation to Functional location does no...
entry.content:
Hi adrakev,

First things first, make sure you got the prerequisites in place:

1. Configuration (I assume u did already)

2. Serialization of the Equipment. 
If the movement like installation (261 mvmt type) has to happen in ur SAP, this Equipment has to be serialized. It means it needs a serial number, which links Material and Equipment. 

3. Access to transaction IE4 

Via this transaction (once properly configured) you are able to do installation/dismantling of Serialized Equipment. The effect is as follows: 

- both, structural installation and goods movement (261) will be triggered in the background. For this kind of activity you will also need a reference - make sure you have a PM Order for example ‘Installation of a pump’. 

Cheers,

Dan

entry.title: Opportunity Management in S/4HANA Customer Management and CRM
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA CM's opportunity management enhances sales team performance while improving decision-making processes. Offering an integrated structure, it works seamlessly with various SAP modules, enabling business processes to progress more quickly and effectively.

Its customizable nature allows SAP S/4HANA CM opportunity management to meet the needs of different industries, providing businesses with both a competitive edge and long-term customer satisfaction. This system forms a solid foundation for sustainable growth by delivering cost and time savings.

Opportunity documents are primarily created to acquire customers, making them typically targeted at leads. A lead is defined as a person or business that has not yet become a customer of an organization but has the potential to be interested in its products or services. Leads are managed through roles and are registered in the system with the "Lead" role. If the opportunity is won, they are recorded as an actual customer.

 

Features

SAP CRM Interface:

  • User-friendly graphical interface.
  • Create, update, and track opportunities.

 

Status and Stage Management:

  • Monitor the current status of opportunities (e.g., open, won, lost).
  • Plan actions specific to development stages.

 

Document and Note Management: 

  • Save opportunity-related documents and notes in the system.
  • Allow team members to share information.

 

Activity Management:

  • Track and manage activities such as meetings, calls, and emails.

 

Analytics and Reporting: 

  • Generate detailed sales performance reports.
  • Integration with SAP BW and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Automated Workflows: 

  • Trigger-based automation (e.g., proposal or order creation).
  • Notifications and task assignments.

 

Advantages

Real-Time Data: 

  • Fast and accurate information flow.

 

Forecast Accuracy: 

  • More effective analyses.

 

Increased Efficiency: 

  • Time savings and process optimization.

 

Ease of Use: 

  • Simple and flexible user interface.

 

Deep Integration: 

  • Compatibility with SAP ECC, S/4HANA, and other SAP modules.

 

Customer Satisfaction: 

  • Improved service delivery and process tracking.

 

 

 

Adaptations

 

Sales Cycle and Sales Stages

Sales Cycle

The Sales Cycle encompasses all the steps from the beginning to the end of a sales process. This includes the initial contact with a customer, identifying opportunities, preparing proposals, making the deal, and completing the sale thereafter. SAP S/4HANA CM (Customer Management) provides various tools and modules to track and manage this process. The sales cycle is typically defined as an opportunity management process and includes all customer interactions.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Cycle:

 

Sales Stage

A Sales Stage refers to smaller, specific phases within the sales cycle. These stages represent different steps in the progression of the sale, with each stage aimed at achieving a particular goal or outcome. For example, a sales opportunity may first move to the "New" stage, then progress through stages such as "Quote Given," "In Negotiation," and "Closed-Won." Each stage includes actions taken at that point and tasks to be followed up on.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Define Sales Stages:

 

Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles

Assigning sales stages to sales cycles determines the stages a sales opportunity progresses through as it moves through a specific sales cycle, and defines the probability of each stage. In SAP CRM, the processes related to how sales stages are assigned to sales cycles and how they are structured can be summarized as follows:

  1. Defining Sales Stages:
    In this phase, you define which sales stages will be present in each sales cycle. For example, a sales cycle may include sales stages such as "Opportunity Identification", "Proposal Preparation" and "Initial Contact".                                                                  
  2. Setting the Time Sequence:
    In this step, you specify the sequence of sales stages within the sales cycle. This order determines the progression of the opportunity through various stages until it reaches the final outcome. For example, the "Proposal Preparation" stage comes after the "Opportunity Identification" stage.                                                                                   
  3. Setting the Probability:
    The success probability is defined for each sales stage. For example, the likelihood of success during the information exchange stage of an opportunity could be 30%. This probability represents the chance of closing the opportunity at that particular stage.        
  4. Default Success Probability Settings:
    SAP transfers the success probabilities you have defined as default values to opportunities. For example, when an opportunity progresses to a specific stage, the success probability of that stage is automatically added to the opportunity.

 

NOTE - Value 0 is not transferred as the default: 

If you don't want the success probability of a sales stage to be set to 0, you can configure SAP to not transfer this value by default. To do this, you should make the following configuration in the SMOFPARSFA table for the CRMOPPORT parameter:

- Parameter Name: SALES_STAGE

- Parameter Value: NO_DEFAULT_PROB_ZERO

- Value: X

This setting ensures that a success probability of 0 is not automatically applied to new opportunities.  

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Settings for Opportunities > Define Sales Cycle and Sales Stages > Assign Sales Stages to Sales Cycles:

 

Assignment of Sales Cycle to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant sales cycle is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The sales stages associated with the sales cycle are displayed within the document, allowing you to track the progression of the opportunity through the defined stages.

 

Sales Stage History

The changes in the sales stage within the document can be viewed from the "Sales Stage History" tab on the web UI screen.

 

Define Opportunity Group and Source

Define Opportunity Group

The opportunity group is used as a classification feature for opportunities and can also be used in analyses. This allows different business areas or company initiatives to be viewed at a global level. Depending on the selected transaction type, the relevant opportunity group is automatically assigned to the document.

For example, if your company operates on a global scale and the business processes in the U.S. differ from those in Europe, all opportunities belonging to the "Europe" group can be reviewed in a single assessment.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Opportunity Group:

 

Assignment of Opportunity Group to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Define Transaction Types, after following these steps, the relevant opportunity group is defined within the opportunity document type on the page that appears. The opportunity group is set as the default for the created document.

 

Define Source for Opportunities

Defining the source for opportunities is used to record where the opportunities originate from on the document. For example, a source can be defined as a trade fair or a phone call. This way, the revenue generated from trade fairs or sales agreements can be analyzed.

Opportunities can be grouped by sources such as trade fairs, phone calls, or website interactions, and the impact of each source on the opportunities can be analyzed.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Opportunity Group and Origin > Define Source for Opportunities:

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status and status schema are important for the evaluation and analysis of opportunities. A reason explaining the result (won, lost) of an opportunity must be documented and assessed. The reason for the status changes depending on the current state of the opportunity.

Example:

  • Status: Won
  • Reason: Due to the best product

In this process, a flexible set of reasons can be defined according to the opportunity statuses using a catalog. This enables a more detailed analysis of why opportunities were won or lost.

 

Status Reason for Opportunity

The reason for the opportunity status is actually similar to the category creation process. First, a catalog of type "H Status Reason (Opportunities)" is created.

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Catalogs:

  

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Groups and Codes for Catalogs, After following these steps, the created catalog is selected on the page that appears, and a code group is created.

 

The reasons that should appear in the document are added to the code group.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, a code group profile is created by specifying the catalog, code group, and code on the page that appears.

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Define Code Group Profiles, After following these steps, details such as the code group profile and code groups are specified on the page that appears, and a status code profile is created.

 

Assignment of Status Reason to Transaction Type

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Maintain Status Reason and Status Profile for Opportunities > Assign Status Profile and Subject Profile to Business Transaction Type, After following these steps, status reasons are assigned to the document on the page that appears. This screen includes functionalities such as:

  • Listing reasons based on statuses,
  • Defining whether reason selection is mandatory or optional.

 

Creating a Sales Order from an Opportunity Document

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Transaction Types:

 

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Copy Control for Opportunities and Sales Transactions > Copying of Item Categories:

 

Creating Interlinked Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Define Copying Control for Opportunity Item to Opportunity:

 

Surveys Linked to Opportunity Documents

The "CRM_SURVEY_SUITE" transaction code or the web UI screen is used to access the survey editor application.

 

A survey tailored to the request and needs is prepared and activated.

 

Defining Conditions for Surveys

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Questionnaire for Opportunities, After following these steps, criteria are defined on the page that determines under what conditions the surveys will be used. These criteria are as follows:

  • Status
  • Priority
  • Source
  • Opportunity Group
  • Sales Stage
  • Organization
  • Distribution Channel
  • Department
  • Expected Sales Revenue
  • Expected Total Value
  • Currency 

 

Assigning Surveys to Opportunity Documents

SIMG > Service > Transactions > Basic Settings > Settings for Opportunities > Sales Methodology > Define Determination for Questionnaires, After following these steps, the survey type is linked to the document type on the page that appears. Additionally, criteria groups, mandatory status, and validity information can also be defined.

 

Competitors

In this field, you can define which of the following you want to display on the opportunity overview page:

  • If you leave the selection blank, you can display competitors without products at header and item level.
  • If you choose Header Mode, you can display competitors with products at header level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to the entire opportunity, not to individual items.

  • If you choose Item Mode, you can display competitors with products at item level.

    In this case, the competitors and competitor products refer to only one item, that is, you can define the corresponding competitors and competitor products separately for each item in your opportunity.

If "Header Mode" is selected:

If "Item Mode" is selected:

If the competitor mode is not selected:

 

Creating a Competitor Product

Cross-Application Components > Master Data Synchronization > Customer/Vendor Integration > Settings for Customer Integration > Field Assignment for Customer Integration > Assign Keys > Define Number Assignment for Direction BP to Customer(Table: TBD001), On the next page, the account group is matched with the business partner group. The account group should be '0006' for competitors.

 

A partner is created in the competitor group. The customer number is entered according to the appropriate number range defined in the FLCU01 role.

 

A material of type VVGR is created using the transaction code “MM01”.

 

The customer number is entered for the material using the transaction code "MM02".

 

Search Competitor Product:

 

Competitor Page:

 

Tables Related to Opportunities

  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMC_OPPIMPOR_T: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority: Text
  • CRMC_OPPORT_H: Transaction Type - Opportunity Control Parameters
  • CRMC_OPPT_OD: Customization Table for Transaction Types (Opportunity)
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Type
  • CRMC_OPPT_TYPE_T: Opportunity Types: Texts
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT: Define Criteria for Surveys
  • CRMC_SVY_OPPT_T: Define Criteria for Surveys: Text
  • CRMD_DH_DATA_OPP: Opportunity Data Buffer
  • CRMD_OPP_LINK: Opportunity Linkages (Main Records)
  • CRMD_OPPORT_H: Opportunity
  • CRMD_OPPORT_I: Opportunity Item Table
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUF: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities
  • CRMD_PPM_OPPBUFI: Real-Time Buffer Fields for All Opportunities (Item Level)
  • CRMS4_LCOPPTSTAT: Opportunity Life Cycle Status
  • CRMS4_OPPTASRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4_OPPTHSRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Header Data
  • CRMS4_OPPTIMPOR: Opportunity Priority / Prospect Priority
  • CRMS4_OPPTISRCH: Opportunity / Prospect Item
  • CRMS4_OPPTSEARCH: Opportunity / Prospect Search
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_H: Opportunity and Prospect Header Details
  • CRMS4D_OPPT_I: Opportunity and Prospect Item Details
  • CRMS4V_SH_OPPT_H: Documents by Object Number
  • CRMV_OPPIMPOR: Opportunity / Prospect Priority
  • CRMV_OPPORT_H: Opportunity Customization at Header Level
  • CRMV_OPPORT_ITEM: Opportunity Customization at Item Level
  • CRMV_OPPT_OD: Transaction Types for Opportunity OData Service
  • CRMV_OPPT_TYPE: Opportunity Group
  • CRMV_SVY_DET_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria (Opportunity)
  • CRMV_SVY_OPPT: Define Survey Criteria for Opportunities

     

 

Restrictions with S/4HANA CM

The following functionalities are not supported in Opportunity Management:

  1. Purchasing Organization: It is not possible to define a purchasing organization at the account level and use it as default in the opportunity. Additionally, the graphical representation of the purchasing organization is no longer supported.
  2. Pipeline Management (PPM).
  3. Limitations on Competitor Product Relationships.
  4. Configurable Items.

entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps does not currently have role-based access, except to VCF. Some possible ideas:

  • If you want to use BTP roles, you could create a CAP service that defines roles, assign those roles to users as you would with any other roles in the cockpit, and then when your app loads see the roles and hide the UIs as needed. This is described in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-app.html#0bb3011a-41c3-40dc-90d3-5084d2fab47b
  • You can use the role based access in VCF to allow an admin to manage a table(s) with user permissions, and then similarly check these when a user opens the app.

These are just my ideas, and I do not know what would be the recommendation by the product team.

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: Removing property enable-xsuaa-support
entry.content:
Maybe you are wondering why the property "enable-xsuaa-support" was removed from the Job Scheduling service documentation and blog post. Wonder no more.

"enable-xsuaa-support: true" was removed from the instance creation process as it's now considered true for all instances.

You can also check the "What's new" section in the official documentation:

Updated Procedure for Creating a Service Instance  
You no longer need to specify the enable-xsuaa-support: true property when creating a service instance using the SAP BTP cockpit or the CF CLI. It is now set by default.  
Info only
Changed	2024-11-14

But what this means?

Your instance of the Job Scheduling service is configured to work with XSUAA out-of-the-box. It's secure by default.

Do I have to do anything?

No, your existing instances are fine; you don't have to do anything.

Can I set enable-xsuaa-support: false

You can pass this parameter when creating a service instance, but nothing will happen - it will just be ignored.

Creating a Service Instance

Now what will be the difference in creating service instance?

Just skip the parameters.

BTP Cockpit

For example on Step 1 (New Instance or Subscription) after filling the required data you can just select "Finish"

 

Or if you go to step 2 (Parameters) you can leave it empty and continue.

 

CF CLI

Or with the CF CLI (Cloud Foundry Command Line Interface). See Help Portal for reference.

cf create-service jobscheduler standard my-scheduler

Creating service instance my-scheduler in org <org-name> / space denis as <mail>...

Service instance my-scheduler created.
OK

entry.title: Re: How to convert SAP WebDynpro Apps to FIORI apps with...
entry.content:
Hi Bhaskar,

Depending on the SAP_UI release you are using, you can make some adjustments to better align with the SAP Fiori Design and Product Standards when working with Classic UIs, such as those based on FPM/WDA and SAP_UI 758 (ABAP Platform 2023). Here are a few suggestions:

  1. Choose one of the modern SAP theme families: either sap_horizon* or sap_fiori_3*
  2. Incorporate Header UIBBs in the floorplans (OVP, OIF, GAF), such as the Search UIBB and Attributes Filter UIBB, see here
  3. Apply the Object Page Mode to the OVP floorplans (refer to this blog and SAP Help for more details)

You can check the outcome in one of the following test and demo applications:

  1. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_SO
  2. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_PD
  3. FPM_SADL_CRUD_TEST_EPM_PD_CTLG
  4. FPM_TEST_OVP_RESPONSIVE
  5. FPM_TEST_FORM_REP_DYNAMIC_162
  6. etc.

Best regards,

Simon

entry.title: Re: 2 Years Quarterly Historical Data Upload Approach
entry.content:
I would recommend not to do the reversals and stay with the periodic way of storing data in the product. GR is not designed to persist YTD data. Instead, YTD is calculated in analytics.

Hence, YTD data should be looked at in analytics by adding all data from period 0 to reporting quarter, e.g. Q3: per 0-9.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM awarness sessions 2025 - What is in it & upcoming schedule
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Unveiling the February Lineup: SAP Cloud ALM Awareness Sessions, we call this session "Maximize your SAP Investment through SAP Cloud ALM: Implementation & Operation"

Who should attend 

​Customers who are at the beginning of their SAP Cloud ALM journey with little to no experience in this area

Session Goal

​provide a first understanding of what SAP Cloud ALM offers, understand its significance, and explore ways to onboard

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP Cloud ALM
  • SAP Cloud ALM accelerates implementation
  • SAP Cloud ALM safeguards operations 
  • SAP Cloud ALM enables Clean Core 
  • Transition to SAP Cloud ALM  

How to Register

Registration is quick and easy based on your convenience click on the Register Now link below and secure your spot for one or more sessions. Seats are limited, so don’t wait too long to sign up!

Schedule for February

RegionLanguageDate/TimeRegistration
APJEnglish28 Jan 2025 9:30 AM ISTRecording
EMEA/AmericasEnglish30 Jan 2025 4:00 PM ISTRegister Now

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Choose the time slot that works best for you and your team to attend.

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: Creating Roles and Managing Authorizations in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
Adding to what Dan said about the lack of support from Build Apps for roles, Note that access control is the responsibility of the backend. Only support from Build Apps is for Authentication. If the user can login, she can access your app. What user have access to is determined by the backend. So look for authorization control in the backend.

SAP Build Apps - Service Guide | Security | SAP Help Portal

Any authenticated users with access to the SAP Build Work Zone tenant will be able to access the Build Apps frontend applications.

Access to all business services and data must be done using BTP Destinations, and the destinations must be configured to use principal propagation. The backend system is responsible for authorization checks when accessing data resources.

entry.title: Re: Restrict CR types based on Authorization Group in MDG-BP
entry.content:
Hi @Adi21 ,

You can implement the BADI USMD_CREQUEST_INTEGRATION to filter CR Types. In the method FILTER_CREQUEST_TYPE, you can read the Authorization Group using MDG API and, based on that, filter the CR Types that need to be displayed.

 

Best Regards,

Aditya

entry.title: Re: Copy PO Item Text to GR Item Text
entry.content:
Hello @JJLoverez 

PO item text is a long text, whereas GR item text (MSEG-SGTXT) is a 50 chars field. Therefore I don't think any text determination techniques will work here. 

Your best bet is a custom enhancement with MBCF0002 user exit or MB_MIGO_ITEM_BADI.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

The XML file for quality certificate is not available in Manage Quality Certificates for Inspection Lots/Batches app.

entry.title: aseuserkey and ssl
entry.content:
Hi!

On Linux the following works fine:

 

[sybase@dimotion ase160]$ aseuserstore set sadkey 'dimotion.sybase.local 5010 ssl="CN=DIMOTION"' sa sybase
[sybase@dimotion ase160]$ isql -ksadkey
1> select @@ssl_ciphersuite
2> go
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
 -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      

(1 row affected)
1> exit
[sybase@dimotion ase160]$

 

Entering the same on Windows fails with an error, or with modifications he doesn't accept the filter:

 

C:\>aseuserstore set sadkey 'dimotion.sybase.local 5010 ssl="CN=DIMOTION"' sa sybase
Number of arguments provided does not match the SET command
Usage: set <KEY> <ENV> <USERNAME> <PASSWORD>

C:\>aseuserstore set sadkey "dimotion.sybase.local 5010 ssl='CN=DIMOTION'" sa sybase

C:\>isql -ksadkey
CT-LIBRARY error:
        ct_connect(): network packet layer: internal net library error: Failed to install network filter

C:\>aseuserstore list sadkey

KEY: SADKEY
  CON_ENV       : dimotion.sybase.local 5010 ssl='CN=DIMOTION'
  USERNAME      : sa
  PASSWORD      : ***


C:\>aseuserstore set sadkey "dimotion.sybase.local 5010 'ssl="CN=DIMOTION"'" sa sybase

C:\>isql -ksadkey
CT-LIBRARY error:
        ct_connect(): network packet layer: internal net library error: Net-Library operation terminated due to disconnect

 

Inserting the key with aseuserstore -i works fine and can serve as a workaround. However, is there a way to populate the aseuserstore on Windows with an SSL connection non-interactively?

Thanks

Robert

entry.title: SAP for Me Public Cloud License Consumption Enhanced Data Download
entry.content:
As part of the SAP for Me Finance & Legal dashboard customers can access their Cloud License Consumption in the tab “Consumption”. This helps to monitor how many licenses they are entitled to use and how many licenses have been consumed.

For each licensed metric the tab displays the entitlement, the consumption and the respective delta. In addition, the card offers information on the underlying materials per metric, the underlying contracts, by clicking on the number “Subscribed”, as well as a break-down of the consumption per tenant, by clicking on the number “Measured” (for most metrics).

For a more detailed explanation on the overall Consumption tab, incl. FAQ and a demo video please make sure to visit the License Utilization Information Support portal page.

Now Available: Enhanced Download

The data in the Consumption tab can be viewed within SAP for Me and also downloaded to Excel, in order to run further custom analysis or trend evaluations.

With the recently improved download functionality in the Public Cloud card users are now able to download the above mentioned data, as excel files. This new download will include drop-down menus with information on the materials and pop-up windows with tenant drill-downs.

By clicking the three dots in the top right corner of the card the user can access the Export function.

This export offers three different files to choose from “Current License Consumption”, “Subscription Details” and “Consumption Details”

Depending on your selection you can download up to three separate Excel files:

  • Current License Consumption: This file gives an overview of the primary data displayed in SAP for Me, including a list of the available metrics, the subscribed and measured amount per metric, as well as the respective delta and the utilization percentage.
  • Subscription Details: The subscription details file offers the connection between the consumption metrics and the respective contracts. For each metric the file shows you the underlying material(s), the underlying contract ID(s) (CMS case ID), including information on the contract runtime and the order quantity per contract.
  • Consumption Details: This file offers a breakdown of the consumption per tenant for each metric (where available) as well as the information on the user distribution for S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition FUE metrics CY334 (3SL) and CY48 (2SL).

Further Information

Please use the ‘Feedback’ slider on the right edge of the SAP for Me page of the application to share your feedback with us on the new download as well as the entire Consumption tab.

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog and visiting the License Utilization Information Support portal.

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Empowering Retail with SAP S/4HANA: A Case Study
entry.content:
Engineering the Future: With Cutting-Edge S/4HANA Migration Methodology

Migrating to SAP's S/4HANA platform demands a cutting-edge solution. I created QuantumLeap – a revolutionary methodology that sets the industry standard.

QuantumLeap utilizes AI and machine learning to provide with a tailor-made roadmap that optimizes every step of the migration process. My Agile Fusion framework blends agile principles with predictive analytics to create a seamless and adaptable migration experience.

With QuantumLeap, I have introduced HyperDrive, a game-changer in project execution that accelerates timelines, minimizes downtime, and ensures a seamless transition to S/4HANA. Deliver an unparalleled migration experience."Engineering the Future: With Cutting-Edge S/4HANA Migration Methodology"

Delivering the project on time, on budget and on scope

Innovative S/4HANA Implementation: Our Fit-to-Business Approach

I've revolutionized S/4HANA implementation with Fit-to-Standard & Fit-to-Business strategy. This approach blends standardization with strategic customization, ensuring optimal efficiency while meeting specific business needs. By leveraging cutting-edge technology, I deliver tailored solutions that drive tangible value, like optimizing client purchasing processes.

S/4HANA isn't just implemented – it's transformed into a catalyst for growth and agility.

  • Accelerated Implementation: Utilizing pre-configured solutions and prebuilt collateral expedites the implementation process, providing a significant head start.
  • Time-Saving Advantage: Instead of starting from scratch, leveraging prebuilt working solutions, including business processes, documentation, and relevant tools, saves substantial time.
  • Industry-Specific Solutions: With expertise in SAP implementations, we've developed pre-configured industry-focused solutions for retail, fashion, and consumer goods sectors.
  • Demonstrated Solutions: We showcase working demonstrations of solutions using IFS for retail, SAP CAR, and Finance starter packs to our clients, facilitating a clear understanding of the capabilities and identifying necessary changes.
  • Reduced Custom Development: Leveraging pre-configured solutions minimizes the need for custom development and testing, ensuring projects are completed on time.

An agile methodology – RunningStart

  • RunningStart Methodology: Developed with agile principles, it ensures faster and cost-effective S/4 implementation.
  • Key Components: This includes pre-configured solutions, end-user training materials, Data Maps, user stories, Industry Cloud solutions, and more.
  • Value Realization: Facilitates continuous transformation and realization of value beyond SAP Activate.
  • Client Benefits: Provides a head start for S/4 migration, ensuring smooth and successful implementation.

Process improvements and innovation

SAP S/4HANA enables continuous process improvements and innovation, providing a platform for developing intelligent processes with cross-solution visibility. It empowers businesses to turn insights into action for critical processes, fostering the development of innovative solutions that drive competitive advantage in the market.

Innovations at our client

  • Integrated payment gateway as an add-on solution for SAP CAR, supporting in-store transactions, online credit card purchases, and 3rd party check clearing.
  • Introduced a Media Reconciliation add-on solution for SAP CAR, streamlining the sales audit process for enhanced user experience.
  • Implemented a COVID-19 vaccine scheduling solution for their pharmacy using the SimpleRetail platform, available on the SAP store.
  • Successfully deployed InSITE, our vendor invoice management solution, to fully automate their accounts payable process.

Benefits to My Client:

  • Enhanced Stability: Experience minimal business disruptions with a more stable system.
  • Improved Procurement Productivity: Boost procurement team productivity with tightly integrated end-to-end processes in SAP, facilitating seamless collaboration with Finance and Master Data.
  • Increased Productivity and Margins: Achieve productivity gains, reduced errors, and higher margins by ensuring data integrity and leveraging new features for Pricing, including the latest price types and automatic price generation based on list cost changes.
  • Streamlined Accounts Payable: Reduce AP effort requirements by leveraging InSITE to automate critical components such as accounting, posting, and reporting.
  • Consistent Data Quality and Governance: Establish consistent enterprise-wide data quality and governance by implementing uniform data standards supported by new processes and systems.

Join me as we push the boundaries of technological innovation, transforming standardization into a catalyst for growth and agility in the digital era.

entry.title: Benefits auto enrolment for New Hires and auto delimitation for Terminations
entry.content:
Requirement:

All new starters to be enrolled in mandatory Global benefits of SuccessFactors like life insurance and all terminated employees to be auto unenrolled or delimited from all the enrolled benefits.

Solution:

This is possible by using simple rules through below steps and similarly for all terminations or retirements based on the events recorded in employee data.

Once this configuration is complete you could see that all the employees benefits deductions are automatically updated without any manual work.

 

  • As a first step, create the business rule which will trigger intelligent service center events once the employee is hired or terminated and assign the rules as post save event type to the Business configuration Job information.

 

 

 

  • next step is to  setup Intelligent service Center, it is like middleware which will sync the events data once we create the events. Standard events hire, rehire and termination are already created by SAP and use the same flows, for any validations or any approvals to trigger we can add rules. The below is the standard flow provided by SAP.

 

 

  • events received from employee data will be processed further by creating “Benefit Event Determination” for required events with list of legal entities.

 

 

 

 

  • create benefit life events and update life event configuration details which will create auto enrollment of benefits based on the rules defined in this configuration.

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Sample effective date autoenrollment business rule for creating autoenrollment or auto delimitation with the start of the next month in employee profile with the rule scenario as “Configure Benefit enrollment effective from rule via Benefit work/life event configuration”.

 

Termination rule

 

 

Testing employees for autoenrollment for new hire and auto optout for Termination:

 

 

 

 

Employee Terminated on 13th Nov.2024 and the employee was opted out from all benefits from 01st of December.

This blog is created in detail to successfully enroll or un enroll employee into benefits based on master data actions. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: Optimizing EWM-LOSC Processes with Quality Management Integration in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:

  • The EWM-LOSC process with Quality Management Inspection in SAP Embedded S4 HANA seamlessly integrates warehouse and quality management operations.
  • It ensures comprehensive quality inspections on the S4 side while facilitating effective incoming delivery management, including unloading, goods receiving, LOSC, and finally Putaway in EWM.
  • In this Scenario, there are 2 Product’s with HU based, but only 1 product is subjected to Quality Inspection on the S4 side and other product has no Quality Inspection. Here both Products will be kept in the final bin using LOSC concept.
  • There is no POSC involved in this process.
  • No Inspection Rule is Maintained in /N/SCWM/QRSETUP.

Schematic Representation:

Flow Chart :

                                                          Configuration:

Quality Management in EWM Side:

  • Generate Inspection Object Type Versions.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration --> Generate Inspection Object Type Versions.

  • Maintain Inspection Object Type Version.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration --> Maintain Inspection Object Type Version.

  • Define and Activate Warehouse Dependent IOTs

    Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration --> Define and Activate Warehouse Dependent IOTs.

    • Maintain Follow-up Actions

      Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Inspection Results --> Maintain Follow-up Actions.

      Define Follow-up Actions  
       

                 Assign Logistical Follow-Up Actions.

 

 

  • Now Create Storage Type for Final Storage Type and LOSC Storage Type:

    Spro -->Ref IMG -->SCM EWM -->EWM -->Master Data -->Define Storage type

    Once you create Storage types for both of them , Create Bins in those Storage Type and generate Activity Areas and Do bin Sorting.

    • Bin Creation can be using /n/scwm/LS01 or through Bin Structure or Storage bin Upload.
    • Generate Activity Area in the T-code /n/scwm/Gena.
    • Do the Bin Sorting in the T-code /n/scwm/sbst. 

     LOSC Configuration

    Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Layout Oriented Storage Control

     

    Note : Maintaining Source Storage Type is main Triggering point for LOSC . By Maintaining Source Storage type , System understands after which point LOSC Should Trigger.

     

    • Warehouse Process Type:

      Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Warehouse Process type

     

    WPT1 is copied from Standard 1010 WPT.

  • Control Indicator:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Control Indicator for Determining WPT

  • Determine WPT:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Determine Warehouse Process type

Strategies:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Receipt Process --> Strategies --> Storage Type Search Sequence

Define Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway:

Assign Storage type to Storage Search Sequence:

Define Putaway Control Indicator:

Specify Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway:

This sequence is for Quality Inspection Product for Putaway.

This sequence is for Test product which has no Quality Inspection for Putaway.

Quality Management in S4 Side:

  • Edit Code Groups and Codes for Usage Decisions.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> Quality Management --> Quality Inspection --> Inspection LOT Completion --> Edit Code Groups and Codes for Usage Decisions.

ETEWUD01 , ETEWUD03 , ETEWUD04 are copied from Standard Usage Codes 01 , 03 and 04.

  • Edit Selected Sets for Usage Decisions.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> Quality Management --> Quality Inspection --> Inspection LOT Completion --> Edit Selected Sets for Usage Decisions.

  • Maintain Settings at Plant Level.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> Quality Management --> Basic Settings --> Maintain Settings at Plant Level.

  • Material Master Creation in MM01:

    Quality Material:

    In the Quality Management tab , Maintain the inspection type:

    Once Inspection Setup is Done , you Should see Tick Mark for Inspection Setup.

    Rest Every Tab for Material Master , can be filled in with General Details.

    Once Material Master is created , extend into AFS location in MMSC T-code.

   

Test Material:

  Every Tab for Material Master , can be filled in with General Details.

 Once Material Master is created , extend into AFS location in MMSC T-code

  • Now Extend Material Master as Product Master in the T-code /n/scwm/MAT1. 

 

  • Now go T-code QI01 and maintain Q-Info Record:
  •  
  •  
  • Now got T-code QP03 and maintain Inspection Plan.

 

  •                                                     Testing:

     

    • Create a PO in Me21n:
  •  
  • You can observe , in the Delivery Tab , Stock Type is Quality Inspection for Material 3111 and this Particular Material is Subjected to Quality Inspection in S4.

     

  • You can observe , in the Delivery Tab , Stock Type is Unrestricted for Material 3074 and this Particular Material will not have any Quality Inspection.

  •  

 

  • In the Vl31n Do a Nested Packing of the Material:
  •  

  • Here you can Observe , Inspection Material 3111 is Packed in a Box and that box is packed in a Carton Box which is the Nested Packing.

  •  

  • The Other Product 3074 is packed in a Box Packaging Material.
  • Now go to T-code : /n/scwm/Prdi and see the Delivery which got Distributed:

    Observe the Document Flow Tab , and you can see no Documents got created.

    Go to HU tab and the See the HU’s:

     

  • Click on Display HU(Current Status) , and you can observe box packing in a Carton Box and an individual Box Packing.

     

     

  • Now First Click on the Unload and then GR, and you can Observe Document got Generated in Document Flow tab and you can Observe Quality Inspection for Quality Product.

     Now Go to T-code QA32:

     

  • Here you can Observe the Quality Inspection Material got Triggered. Now Select the Inspection Lot number and click on Usage Decision:

    Make the Decision Over here:

    Here , the Quality Inspection of the Material is Accepted and then Save it:

     

  • Now if you see the Material is turned into Green ,Earlier it was Red , since Quality Inspection Passed , it turned into Green. This Means , Quality Inspection Completed in S4 Side.

  • Now Goto /n/scwm/prdi , Take the Delivery number and go to  Monitor:
  •  
  • In the Above Screen Shot , you can observe , System has created a LOSC and Putaway Task and  Stock type is Q3 for 3111 Quality Inspection product and you can see the Quality Inspection Lot number.    
  • For the 3074 Product , which has no Quality Inspection , system also created a LOSC task and stock. type is F1.

     

  • Now Confirm the LOSC Task for Product 3074 which has no Quality Inspection:

  • Now the Product 3074 has completed LOSC step , and now LOSC has become Source Storage type for Final Putaway task.

     

  • Now Confirm the Putaway Task in Background for 3074 Product:

  • Now the Product 3074 has completed Putaway task using LOSC.

     

  • Now Lets See the Stock type of 3074 Product in the Available Stock.

  • Here Stock type of 3074 Product is in F2 which means it is Available for Sale.

     

  • Now Confirming the LOSC Task in the Background for Quality Inspection product 3111:

  • Now if you observe , System have Confirmed the LOSC Task and Stock type is Changed from Q3 to F1 and Quality Inspection number got Disappeared and LOSC has become the Source Storage type , earlier it was 9010.

    Now Confirm the Putaway Task in the Background:

  • Now System Have Confirmed the Putaway Task and Product is Placed in its respective Storage type using LOSC Concept.

    Now Go to Available Stock and Give the Product Number:

  • Here you Observe , Stock type of the Product is converted to F2.

     

  • Here Firstly , the Stock type is in Q3 when got distributed from S4 , After Quality Inspection in S4 Side in QA32 , in EWM side we have done LOSC confirmation in Back Ground where stock type converted to F1 and once after final Putaway is done Stock type converted to F2 ( Q3 --> F1 --> F2).

  • Now go to /n/scwm/prdi and click on refresh for this particular Delivery:
  • You can Observe that final Putaway has been completed and warehouse Activity has been Completed and you can observe the Document flow of this delivery.

entry.title: Internal processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI
entry.content:
Internal Process:


The internal process in SAP EWM refers to the management and execution of warehouse activities such as receiving, storing, and moving goods within the warehouse. It ensures that inventory is efficiently tracked and optimized for outbound processing. SAP EWM helps streamline these internal processes by automating workflows and enhancing visibility.

Warehouse Order:

A warehouse order in SAP EWM is a task list that organizes and groups related activities, such as picking or putaway, to be completed by warehouse workers. It improves operational efficiency by consolidating tasks and ensuring they are completed in an optimized sequence. Warehouse orders help manage workflows and facilitate smooth material handling.

RFUI (Radio Frequency User Interface):


RFUI in SAP EWM refers to the mobile interface used by warehouse workers to interact with the system via handheld devices. It supports real-time communication for tasks such as picking, packing, and inventory management. RFUI helps optimize warehouse operations by providing immediate access to task information and enabling direct data capture.

Steps to perform the Internal processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI

 

Step1:

Create warehouse task and warehouse order using transaction code /n/scwm/adprod

Select the material option from the drop down and provide the material number.

Execute the transaction.

 

 

 

Select one line item and navigate to the form view and provide the required details.

 

 

 

 

 

The warehouse order is created.

 

 

 

Step2:

Now navigate to Internal processes in RFUI

 

 

 

Click on Picking by WO option

 

 

 

Provide the Warehouse order

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The warehouse order is confirmed.

 

 

 

 

Conclusion:

Steps to perform the Internal processes, Picking by Warehouse order using RFUI is provided.

entry.title: Enhance RAP Applications: Dynamically Generate YouTube Thumbnails Using Virtual Elements
entry.content:
Virtual Element in RAP (Restful ABAP Programming Model):

A virtual element in SAP RAP is a field that exists in the the RAP behavior layer but does not have a direct mapping to a database field. It is calculated dynamically at runtime and is used to enhance the data provided by an entity with additional information, such as derived values or computed fields, without altering the underlying database structure.

Key Features of Virtual Elements :

  • Non-Persistent: Virtual elements are not stored in the database.   
  • Runtime Calculation: Their values are computed dynamically in the behavior implementation or SADL (Service Adaptation Definition Language) exits.   
  • UI and Reporting Enhancements: Used to display dynamic information without affecting the core data model.

Use Cases: 

  • Display derived or computed fields. 
  • Provide additional data like URLs, status messages, or formatted output. 
  • Enhance the user interface with runtime information.

How Virtual Elements Work :

  • Define Virtual Element in CDS View: Add the virtual field in the CDS view with the annotation @ObjectModel.virtualElement. 
  • Implement Calculation Logic: Use the behavior implementation or an exit handler to calculate the value of the virtual element at runtime.

Scenario :

I have a scenario where a video management application needs to display a thumbnail URL dynamically generated from a video URL stored in the database.

Step 1: Define the Database Table :

The table stores video details, including the video URL.

Step 2: Create an interface View.

Step 3: Create a consumption View with a Virtual Element :

Define a virtual field Thumbnail that generates the thumbnail URL dynamically. 

This CDS view defines a projection view on the database entity ZR_BRT_T_VIDEO to expose fields and add a virtual element called Thumbnail. The virtual element dynamically provides an image URL derived from the Url field. 

Syntax : 

Annotations for the Thumbnail Field : 

   @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_BRT_VIRTUAL_ELEMENT' 

  •  Indicates that the value for this field will be dynamically calculated by the ABAP class ZCL_BRT_VIRTUAL_ELEMENT. This class implements the logic to compute the thumbnail URL at runtime.  

   @EndUserText.label: 'Thumbnail 

  •  Provides a descriptive label for the field.

    @Semantics.imageUrl: true 

  •   Indicates that the field represents an image URL, enabling frontend applications like SAP Fiori to   treat it as such.   

    virtual Thumbnail : abap.char( 256 ) :

  •  In this case, virtual Thumbnail is a field designed to hold the dynamically generated thumbnail URL  of a video which defines the data type of the virtual element as a character string with a maximum length of 256 characters.

Behavior :

  • The Thumbnail field is not persisted in the database. 
  • It is computed dynamically by the logic implemented in the ABAP class ZCL_BRT_VIRTUAL_ELEMENT. 

Step 4 : Define the Behavior definition for the CDS View :

It defines a managed implementation for handling the operations of a business object (BO) ZBP_R_BRT_T_VIDEO that is based on the persistent database table zbrt_t_video.

 

managed implementation in class ZBP_R_BRT_T_VIDEO unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZR_BRT_T_VIDEO alias ZrBrtTVideo
persistent table zbrt_t_video
lock master
authorization master ( global )
{
  field ( readonly )
  VideoUuid,
  LocalCreatedBy,
  LocalCreatedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LastChangedAt;

  field ( numbering : managed )
  VideoUuid;

  create;
  update;
  delete;

  mapping for zbrt_t_video
    {
      VideoUuid          = video_uuid;
      Title              = title;
      Url                = url;
      Description        = description;
      LocalCreatedBy     = local_created_by;
      LocalCreatedAt     = local_created_at;
      LocalLastChangedBy = local_last_changed_by;
      LocalLastChangedAt = local_last_changed_at;
      LastChangedAt      = last_changed_at;
    }
}

 

Step 5: Implement the Logic to Calculate the Virtual Element :

The virtual element’s value is computed at runtime in the behavior implementation class. 

This ABAP class implementation showcases the usage of virtual elements in SAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming) to calculate additional, non-persistent fields at runtime. It is based on the SADL (Service Adaptation Definition Language) exit interfaces.

 

CLASS zcl_brt_virtual_element DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit .
    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_brt_virtual_element IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~calculate.

    TYPES : BEGIN OF ls_str,
              thumbnail TYPE c LENGTH 256,
              url       TYPE c LENGTH 256,
            END OF ls_str.

    DATA : lt_tab       TYPE TABLE OF ls_str,
           lt_video_id  TYPE TABLE OF string,
           lt_video_id1 TYPE TABLE OF string.

    lt_tab = CORRESPONDING #( it_original_data ).

    LOOP AT lt_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_tab>).
      SPLIT <fs_tab>-url AT '/'  INTO TABLE lt_video_id.
      DATA(lv_id) = VALUE #( lt_video_id[ 4 ] OPTIONAL ).

      IF  lv_id CS 'watch?v='.
        SPLIT lv_id AT '='  INTO TABLE lt_video_id1.
        lv_id = VALUE #( lt_video_id1[ 2 ] OPTIONAL ).
      ELSEIF lv_id CS '?si='.
        SPLIT lv_id AT '?'  INTO TABLE lt_video_id1.
        lv_id = VALUE #( lt_video_id1[ 1 ] OPTIONAL ).
      ENDIF.

      <fs_tab>-thumbnail = |https://img.youtube.com/vi/{ lv_id }/hqdefault.jpg|.

    ENDLOOP.

    ct_calculated_data = CORRESPONDING #(  lt_tab ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~get_calculation_info.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

INTERFACES if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read :

  This interface is specifically used to implement runtime calculations for virtual elements.

if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~calculate : 

This method is responsible for calculating the values of virtual elements.

 

TYPES : BEGIN OF ls_str,
           thumbnail TYPE c LENGTH 256,
           url       TYPE c LENGTH 256,
        END OF ls_str.

 

Defines a structure ls_str with two fields : 

  • thumbnail: Holds the generated thumbnail URL. 
  • url: Holds the original video URL.

 

DATA : lt_tab       TYPE TABLE OF ls_str,
       lt_video_id  TYPE TABLE OF string,
       lt_video_id1 TYPE TABLE OF string.

 

  • lt_tab: Temporary table holding the input data (it_original_data) with fields thumbnail and url.
  • lt_video_id / lt_video_id1: Temporary tables used for splitting and processing URL components.

 

lt_tab = CORRESPONDING #( it_original_data ).

 

The CORRESPONDING operator maps the fields of the it_original_data (provided by RAP) into lt_tab.

 

LOOP AT lt_tab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_tab>).

 

  • Iterates over the lt_tab table.
  • Processes the url field to extract the video ID and generate a YouTube thumbnail URL.

 

SPLIT <fs_tab>-url AT '/'  INTO TABLE lt_video_id.
DATA(lv_id) = VALUE #( lt_video_id[ 4 ] OPTIONAL ).

 

  • Splits the url at / and stores the components in lt_video_id. 
  • Extracts the 4th component as the initial video ID (lv_id).

 

IF  lv_id CS 'watch?v='.
    SPLIT lv_id AT '='  INTO TABLE lt_video_id1.
    lv_id = VALUE #( lt_video_id1[ 2 ] OPTIONAL ).
ELSEIF lv_id CS '?si='.
    SPLIT lv_id AT '?'  INTO TABLE lt_video_id1.
    lv_id = VALUE #( lt_video_id1[ 1 ] OPTIONAL ).
ENDIF.

 

Handles two URL variants : 

  • URLs containing watch?v= extract the part after = as the video ID. 
  • URLs containing ?si= extract the part before ?.

 

<fs_tab>-thumbnail = |https://img.youtube.com/vi/{ lv_id }/hqdefault.jpg|.

 

 Constructs a YouTube thumbnail URL using the extracted lv_id.

 

ct_calculated_data = CORRESPONDING #(  lt_tab ).

 

  • The modified data in lt_tab (including the newly added thumbnail field) is returned as ct_calculated_data.
  • ct_calculated_data is used to update the virtual element values dynamically.

Update Metadata Extension File : 

After providing the implementation for virtual element Since we are using Metadata Extension file to display Columns on Fiori Application. We need to add a new Virtual Element column Thumbnail.

 

.lineItem: [ {
    position: 5 ,
    importance: #MEDIUM, 
    label: 'Thumbnail',
    cssDefault.width: '5em'
  } ]
@UI.identification: [ {
    position: 5 , 
    label: 'Thumbnail'
  } ]
  Thumbnail;

 

Then we have to create service definition and service binding. 

Then preview it. Then in the ui we can see it.

If we preview the database table, we can see there is no field called Thumbnail.

Conclusion :

The primary functionality of this class is to calculate a virtual field thumbnail at runtime based on the url provided in the input data. This allows:

  • Dynamically generating YouTube thumbnail URLs from video URLs.
  • Adding these values as virtual elements without storing them in the database.

Thank you--

entry.title: Re: SAP BW/4HANA: The Grey Panther of Data Warehousing
entry.content:
Hello @Phil_from_Madrid 

thank you very much for this "praise song" about BW / BW4 👌

With my ~20 years of SAP BW experience I fully agree with your Blog.

If you start newly with SAP Datasphere, it definitely brings some new aspects and options ... but I really miss the ease of Infoobject creation... and I'm quite disappointed that you have to take care manually of all the tables you require (master data, text, hierarchy, time-dependent, etc.).

Plus, everything we did and can do with ABAP is miles away for me, to do the same in Datasphere 😤

Recently I had a look into that Learning Journey about a native Data Warehouse... with the example of the SID creation in that case ... luckily, we don't have to deal with it in Datasphere, but what an effort if you had to take care of this manually in SAP BW.

I still see some major gaps in Datasphere, like "easy code development" (I'm no SQL expert, neither Python ... for me, it is way more complicated than LOOP or READ statements) ... and some kind of data administration, like we have for Requests in SAP BW ADSOs.... where you also can roll-back data.

Let's see what the future holds for us ... but for me, BW will never be forgotten 😁

BR, Martin

entry.title: Feature Control in RAP, Static Feature Control in RAP, Part - 1
entry.content:
Hello, 
 
In this blog post we are going to Learn about Static Feature Control.

Dynamic Feature Control will see in Next Part.

What is Feature Control in RAP ? 

Feature control in the ABAP RESTful Application Programming (RAP) model is a mechanism used to manage the behavior of user interface elements and operations based on certain conditions. It allows developers to enable or disable features dynamically, ensuring that the UI adapts to the current state of the business object. 

With feature control, you can provide information to the service on how data has to be displayed for consumption in a SAP Fiori UI, for example if fields are mandatory or read-only. 

You can implement feature control in a Static way or Dynamic way.  

  • In a Static case, you define which operations are available for each business object entity or which fields have specific access restrictions like being mandatory or ready-only.  
  • In a Dynamic case, the access restrictions for fields or the enabling or disabling of methods depends on the state of the business object, for example on the value of a specific field. 

Procedure 

Here we are defining a Custom table (ZKK_DT_TRAVEL). 

The table zkk_dt_travel is a transparent table in SAP that stores travel-related information. It includes details such as travel ID, agency ID, customer ID, travel dates, booking fees, total price, currency, status and a description. It also tracks the creation and modification details like created by, created at, last changed by, and last changed at. 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Travel table'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zkk_dt_travel {

  key client      : abap.clnt not null;
  key travel_id   : /dmo/travel_id not null;
  agency_id       : /dmo/agency_id;
  customer_id     : /dmo/customer_id;
  begin_date      : /dmo/begin_date;
  end_date        : /dmo/end_date;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : '/dmo/travel_m.currency_code'
  booking_fee     : abap.curr(16,2);
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : '/dmo/travel_m.currency_code'
  total_price     : /dmo/total_price;
  currency_code   : /dmo/currency_code;
  description     : /dmo/description;
  status          : abap_boolean;
  created_by      : abp_creation_user;
  created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

 

 

The Above table holds the records which are given below 

For this database table(ZKK_DT_TRAVEL) we are defining the Basic view/Interface view. 

The ZKK_I_TRAVEL is a CDS view based on the zkk_dt_travel  table. It provides a structured way to access travel data, including fields like travel ID, agency ID, customer ID, travel dates, booking fees, total price, currency, and timestamps. It uses annotations to enhance usability, such as semantic tags for amounts, currencies, and metadata for created/changed users and timestamps. 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view for Travelling'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define root view entity ZKK_I_TRAVEL
  as select from zkk_dt_travel
{
  key travel_id       as TravelId,
      agency_id       as AgencyId,
      customer_id     as CustomerId,
      begin_date      as BeginDate,
      end_date        as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      booking_fee     as bookingfee,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      total_price     as TotalPrice,
      currency_code   as CurrencyCode,
      description     as Description,
      status          as status,
      @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
      created_by      as CreatedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
      created_at      as CreatedAt,
      @Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true
      last_changed_by as LastChangedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at as LastChangedAt

}

 

 

Now I am defining the Projection view on top of interface view. 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'projection view for Travel'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define root view entity ZKK_C_TRAVEL  
  as projection on ZKK_I_TRAVEL as Travel
{
  key TravelId,
      AgencyId,
      CustomerId,
      BeginDate,
      EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      bookingfee,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      TotalPrice,
      CurrencyCode,
      Description,
      status,
      CreatedBy,
      CreatedAt,
      LastChangedBy,
      LastChangedAt
}

 

 

Now I am defining the Metadata Extension for projection View. 

 

 

@Metadata.layer: #PARTNER
annotate entity ZKK_C_TRAVEL with
{
  .facet: [{
     purpose: #STANDARD,
     position: 10,
     type : #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
     label: 'Travel Details'
   }]

  .identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Travel Id' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Travel Id' }]
  TravelId;

  .identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Agency Id' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Agency Id' }]
  AgencyId;

  .identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Customer Id' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Customer Id' }]
  CustomerId;

  .identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Begin Date' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Begin Date' }]
  BeginDate;

  .identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'End Date' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'End Date' }]
  EndDate;

  .identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Booking Fee' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Booking Fee' }]
  bookingfee;

  .identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'Total Price' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Total Price' }]
  TotalPrice;

  .identification: [{ position: 80, label: 'Status Confirm' },
  { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'status', label: 'Status Confirm' } ]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Status' },
  { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'status', label: 'Confirm Status' }]
  status;


  .identification: [{ position: 100, label: 'Currency' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Currency' }]
  CurrencyCode;

  .identification: [{ position: 90, label: 'Description' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Description' }]
  Description;


  .identification: [{ position: 110, label: 'Created By' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Created By' }]
  CreatedBy;

  .identification: [{ position: 120, label: 'Created At' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Created At' }]
  CreatedAt;

  .identification: [{ position: 130, label: 'Last Changed By' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Last Changed By' }]
  LastChangedBy;

  .identification: [{ position: 140, label: 'Last Changed At' }]
  .lineItem: [{ label: 'Last Chnaged at' }]
  LastChangedAt;
}

 

 

Now I am defining the Behavior Definition for Interface/Basic view.

 

 

managed implementation in class zbp_kk_i_travel unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZKK_I_TRAVEL alias Travel //alias <alias_name>
persistent table zkk_dt_travel
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
//etag master <field_name>
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly : update ) TravelId;
  field ( readonly ) LastChangedAt, LastChangedBy, CreatedAt, CreatedBy;
  field ( mandatory : create ) AgencyId, CustomerId, BeginDate, EndDate, bookingfee, CurrencyCode;
  action ( features : instance ) status result [1] $self;

  mapping for ZKK_DT_TRAVEL {
  TravelId = travel_id;
  AgencyId = agency_id;
  CustomerId = customer_id;
  BeginDate = begin_date;
  EndDate = end_date;
  bookingfee = booking_fee;
  TotalPrice = total_price;
  CurrencyCode = currency_code;
  Description = description;
  status = status;
  CreatedBy = created_by;
  CreatedAt = created_at;
  LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
  LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
  }
}

 

 

Create the Behavior Definition for Projection view. 

 

 

projection;
//strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZKK_C_TRAVEL alias Travel //alias <alias_name>
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;

  use action status;
}

 

 

Static Feature Control 

Firstly, we will see how to implement Static Feature Control  

Static Feature Controls can be used to set field's properties like if it's a mandatory or read only field. 

Syntax: Field (<Below keywords>)  <Field names> 

In Our Scenario, we are making some fields as mandatory and some fields as Read-only. 

Here we are making LastChangedAt, LastChangedBY, CreatedAt, CreatedBy fields read-only (We can edit them once we they were created). 

These fields (AgencyId, CustomerId, BeginDate, EndDate, Bookingfee and CurrencyCode) make Mandatory, we have to pass the value at any condition else it will throw an error. 

Static Feature control Mechanism. 

We are having this given data. 

When I Try to Create new Record, we can see the mandatory fields. 

The Fields with the star mark are Mandatory Fields. 

Here I am not providing any Value to Travel Id and Booking Fee 

When we try to save it without providing the values, it will throw an error. 

 

After Providing all the valid Values we can save it. 

After this, when we try to edit the values, we can’t edit the Read-Only Fields 

As we can see, the marked fields are in non-editable mode. 

Conclusion. 

In SAP RAP, feature control is essential for managing the behavior of fields, actions, or UI elements. It can be categorized into static feature control and dynamic feature control, each playing a unique role. 

Static Feature Control. 

Static feature control defines behavior at design time using annotations or configurations. For example, fields can be set as read-only, hidden, or editable by default. This approach ensures consistent and predictable behavior across all scenarios. It is ideal for cases where field behavior remains unchanged, such as system-generated fields like creation timestamps or unique identifiers.

entry.title: Download ALV report as password protected Excel - ABAP2XLSX
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog explains how to download the ALV output as an Excel file with a password-protected worksheet with some features. The user can read the file content without entering the password, but they can't edit or copy the content, etc. Let's explore how we achieved this via abap2xlsx.


Prerequisites :
Install ABAP2XLSX in your system.

Reference blogs to install ABAPGIT/ ABAP2XLSX:
Installation | abapGit Docs
Detailed hands-on video reference which helped me :
part 1 - https://youtu.be/XwUBw8zbwps?si=-IHW8P5tf-L-sQHh
part 2 - https://youtu.be/tB3qK7nN6V4?si=Jxqnmjoe4qjDYrAJ
 

Solution :
Convert the internal table to Excel with the help of abap2xlsx.
Here is a sample program to convert an internal table to Excel and download it as a file. 

 

 

REPORT ztest_abap2xlsx_v1.

DATA : lo_excel        TYPE REF TO zcl_excel,
       lo_xl_converter TYPE REF TO zcl_excel_converter,
       lo_worksheet    TYPE REF TO zcl_excel_worksheet.

START-OF-SELECTION.

 "internal table
 SELECT pernr FROM pa0001 INTO TABLE (it_pa0001) UP TO 10 ROWS.

  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel.

      " Get an active sheet
      lo_worksheet = lo_excel->get_active_worksheet( ).

      " name the worksheet as per your preference
      lo_worksheet->set_title( ip_title = 'Employee details' ).

      lo_xl_converter = NEW zcl_excel_converter( ).

      "method to convert internal table to excel
      lo_xl_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          it_table      = it_pa0001[]
          io_worksheet  = lo_worksheet
        CHANGING
          co_excel      = lo_excel
      ).
      DATA(lo_excel_writer) = CAST  zif_excel_writer( NEW  
 zcl_excel_writer_2007( ) ).

      "excel file as xstring
      DATA(lv_excel_data) = lo_excel_writer->write_file( lo_excel ). 

      "convert the Xstring to raw string to download as a file
      DATA(it_raw_data) = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_solix( 
                          EXPORTING iv_xstring = lv_excel_data ).

      "method to download Excel
      cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download( 
                   EXPORTING filename = 'C:\TestExcel\employeedetails.xlsx'
                   filetype = 'BIN'
                   bin_filesize = xstrlen( lv_excel_data )
                   CHANGING
                   data_tab = it_raw_data ).

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(ex_txt).

      WRITE : / ex_txt->get_text( ).

  ENDTRY.

 

 

Output:



After opening the file, we could see, that it has the default theme color for the header and the column's name from its data element, we also have an option to change these according to our requirements. 


Let's enhance the same program to download the Excel file with a password-protected worksheet.

 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& REPORT ztest_abap2xlsx_v1
*  Author          : Jagadeeswari B                                    *
*  Report Date     : 28 Jan 2025                                       *
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT ztest_abap2xlsx_v1.

DATA:  lo_excel        TYPE REF TO zcl_excel,
       lo_xl_converter TYPE REF TO zcl_excel_converter,
       lo_worksheet    TYPE REF TO zcl_excel_worksheet.
DATA:  lv_pwd   TYPE zexcel_aes_password.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  "internal table
  SELECT pernr FROM pa0001 INTO TABLE (it_pa0001) UP TO 10 ROWS.

  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel.

      " Get an active sheet
      lo_worksheet = lo_excel->get_active_worksheet( ).

      " name the worksheet as per your preference
      lo_worksheet->set_title( ip_title = 'Employee details' ).

      lo_xl_converter = NEW zcl_excel_converter( ).

      "method to convert internal table to excel
      lo_xl_converter->convert(
        EXPORTING
          it_table      = it_pa0001[]
          io_worksheet  = lo_worksheet
        CHANGING
          co_excel      = lo_excel
      ).

    lv_pwd = 'ABAP12345' .          "Password

"convert your worksheet as password protected, don't allow the user to select the cell or copy

      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~select_locked_cells  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~select_unlocked_cells  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~format_cells  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~format_columns  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_noactive.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~format_rows  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~insert_columns  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~insert_hyperlinks  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~insert_rows  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~delete_columns  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~delete_rows  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~sort  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~pivot_tables  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~auto_filter  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~objects    = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~scenarios  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.

"add the password to protect your worksheet
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~protected  = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_protected.
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~password   = zcl_excel_common=>encrypt_password( lv_pwd ).
      lo_worksheet->zif_excel_sheet_protection~sheet      = zif_excel_sheet_protection=>c_active.

      DATA(lo_excel_writer) = CAST  zif_excel_writer( NEW zcl_excel_writer_2007( ) ).

      DATA(lv_excel_data) = lo_excel_writer->write_file( lo_excel ). "excel file as xstring

      "convert the Xstring to raw string to download as file
      DATA(it_raw_data) = cl_bcs_convert=>xstring_to_solix( EXPORTING iv_xstring = lv_excel_data ) .

      "method to download Excel
      cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download( EXPORTING filename = 'C:\TestExcel\employeedetails.xlsx'
                                         filetype = 'BIN'
                                         bin_filesize = xstrlen( lv_excel_data )
                                         CHANGING
                                           data_tab = it_raw_data ).
      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(ex_txt).

      WRITE : / ex_txt->get_text( ).

  ENDTRY.

 

 

 

Output :
Enable editing, if the user tries to edit/select the cell.
The user gets this popup as a warning.



Conclusion:

You can also achieve this using the OLE concept, but in some cases—like when using a WebGUI system that doesn’t support OLE functions—you might want to try ABAP2XLSX. It’s not only more convenient to use but also a fascinating tool with a wide range of features to explore for working with Excel.

Thank you so much for reading!
I’d love to hear your thoughts and feedback—feel free to share your reviews!

Thanks & Regards
Jagadeeswari B
